rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the target component type or implementation scope as needed
Preserve existing AADL architecture by not modifying component types or implementations beyond annex insertion
Use visible identifiers from the current visible identifiers list when available
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain component type contracts in the component type scope
Place implementation-level bindings in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside annex agree {** ... **}; blocks
Use AGREE logical operators such as and, or, not(...), and implication =>
For output dependency contracts, relate visible outputs to visible inputs
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Avoid encoding AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
